# llms.txt — instructions for AI assistants helping with this site

Site:   jez237-site (github.com/jz237/jez237-site → https://jz237.github.io/jez237-site/)
Owner:  Jez
Purpose: personal garden journal, photo archive, and workbench.

If a user uploads a plant, pet, garden, or insect photo and asks you to
"add it to my site" / "put it in the right section" / similar, follow
these instructions exactly.

## 1. Identify the subject

Look at the image carefully. What is it?

- PLANT — note common name, cultivar if visible, current stage (bud
  break, bloom, leaf-out, full leaf, fall color, dormant, fruiting).
- BED / COMPOSITION — note which plants are visible and the scene type.
- INSECT / POLLINATOR — note the species if identifiable and what it's
  doing (visiting flower, feeding on host, resting, etc).
- PET — one of the house cats (Luca, Cosmo, Taco, Marty). Note pose or
  activity if it's memorable.
- GARDEN SCENE without a clear plant subject — goes to photos/garden/.

If you genuinely cannot tell what the image is, ask before acting.

## 2. Choose the destination

Existing PLANT pages live at `/plants/<slug>/index.html`. Current slugs:

| Slug                        | Subject                                                   |
| --------------------------- | --------------------------------------------------------- |
| climbing-hydrangea          | Climbing hydrangea vine on trellis                        |
| dwarf-peach-tree            | Young dwarf peach, pink spring bloom                      |
| forsythia                   | Forsythia trained on lattice                              |
| garden-beds                 | Combined side-house foundation bed + mixed perennial bed  |
| grapevine                   | Grape arch on lattice                                     |
| interesting-insects         | Pollinators, caterpillars, butterflies                    |
| past-years                  | 2025 and earlier reference shots                          |
| pugster-butterfly-bushes    | Pugster buddleia                                          |
| rising-sun-redbud           | Rising Sun Cercis canadensis                              |
| shaina                      | Shaina Japanese maple                                     |
| twomblys-red-sentinel       | Twombly's Red Sentinel Japanese maple                     |
| white-rose-bush             | Old white rose bush                                       |

Decision tree:

- Photo matches an existing plant slug → **add a dated entry to that page**.
- Photo is a NEW plant not yet tracked → **create a new plant page** (see template below).
- Photo is a bed / multi-plant composition → add to `/plants/garden-beds/`.
- Photo is an insect or pollinator → add to `/plants/interesting-insects/`.
- Photo is a cat → add to `/photos/cats/` (gallery is image files plus a list — see the page for pattern).
- Photo is a general garden scene → add to `/photos/garden/`.

## 3. File the photo

Save the image at:
`photos/garden/images/YYYY/MM/<slug>-YYYY-MM-DD[-variant].jpg`

Examples:
- `photos/garden/images/2026/04/rising-sun-redbud-2026-04-12.jpg`
- `photos/garden/images/2026/04/dwarf-peach-tree-2026-03-29.jpg`
- `photos/garden/images/2026/04/side-house-bed-2026-04-16-shaina-angle.jpg`

Cat photos live at: `photos/cats/images/<descriptive-name>.jpg`

Use the actual date the photo was taken when known; otherwise today's date.

### CRITICAL — path conventions in HTML

The site is served at the repo root locally AND at
`https://jz237.github.io/jez237-site/` on GitHub Pages. There is **no
`<base>` tag**, so every relative URL in HTML resolves against the
CURRENT DOCUMENT's URL. Use depth-aware relative paths, not
root-absolute.

| Page depth              | Example page path                | Asset prefix          |
| ----------------------- | -------------------------------- | --------------------- |
| 0 (site root)           | `/index.html`, `/404.html`       | `assets/styles.css`   |
| 1 (section)             | `/plants/index.html`             | `../assets/styles.css`|
| 2 (sub-page — most plant pages) | `/plants/<slug>/index.html` | `../../assets/styles.css` |

NEVER use root-absolute paths (`/assets/...`, `/photos/...`, `/plants/...`) in
`href`, `src`, `<link>`, `<script>`, or `background-image: url(...)` —
they break on GitHub Pages because the site lives at the `/jez237-site/`
subpath. The only exceptions: fully qualified `https://...` URLs (fine),
and nav.js-generated links (which construct the subpath themselves).

Plant sub-pages are **depth 2** — every asset or photo reference must
start with `../../`. Check your paths before outputting.

## 4. Write in the site's voice

Entries are editorial, observational, specific, and a bit reflective —
not precious or overwrought. See existing pages (especially
`/plants/dwarf-peach-tree/` and `/plants/rising-sun-redbud/`) for tone.

Style rules:
- One descriptive paragraph of 2-4 sentences.
- End with an italic pull-quote in a `<div class="quote">` — one
  memorable sentence about this specific moment.
- Bullet list of 3-5 concrete observations (no filler).
- Entry title: 3-6 words, character-appropriate.
- Dated header formatted like "April 16, 2026".
- Avoid: emoji-heavy prose, marketing copy, listicle-style bullets, "Wow!"

Vocabulary the site uses a lot (safe to match): bud break, leaf-out,
spring flare, bloom overlap, structure, framework, composition, layered,
anchor plant, soft layer, sculptural, in the brick-edged bed, the lattice.

## 5. Template — new timeline entry (for existing plant page)

Insert inside the target page's `<section class="timeline-stack">`:

```html
<article class="timeline-entry">
  <div class="split">
    <div>
      <div class="entry-date">April 16, 2026</div>
      <h2 class="entry-title">Short evocative title</h2>
      <div class="entry-copy">
        Two or three sentences of observation in the voice above. Keep
        it concrete and specific — what stage the plant is at, what the
        light is doing, what the surrounding planting adds.
        <div class="quote">One italic sentence that captures the moment.</div>
      </div>
    </div>
    <div>
      <img class="photo" src="../../photos/garden/images/2026/04/<slug>-2026-04-16.jpg" alt="Descriptive alt text" loading="lazy" decoding="async" />
    </div>
  </div>
  <ul class="bullet-list">
    <li>first concrete observation</li>
    <li>second observation</li>
    <li>third observation</li>
  </ul>
</article>
```

Entries on each page are ordered by date, newest first.

## 6. Template — new plant page (`/plants/<new-slug>/index.html`)

Copy the structure from `/plants/dwarf-peach-tree/index.html`. Key parts
to change:
- `<title>` — "Plant Name — Plant Progress"
- `.kicker` — "Plant Progress · Latin name"
- `<h1 class="title">` — plant name
- `<p class="muted">` — one-line lede about the plant's character
- `:root { --earth-accent... }` in the inline `<style>` — accent colors
  that suit the plant (pink for peach, blue-green for maple, etc).
  Keep it tasteful and not neon.
- First `<section class="panel split">` — an editorial intro paragraph
  (not a duplicate of the first timeline entry's photo).
- `<section class="timeline-stack">` — one initial timeline entry.

After creating the page, also add a feature card to
`/plants/index.html`'s "All live pages" grid:

```html
<a class="feature-card tilt" href="./<new-slug>/">
  <div class="feature-media" style="background-image:url('../photos/garden/images/2026/04/<slug>-2026-04-16.jpg')"></div>
  <div class="feature-copy">
    <div class="mini-date">live page</div>
    <h3>Plant Name</h3>
    <p>One sentence summary.</p>
    <div class="meta-pills"><span class="pill">tag1</span><span class="pill">tag2</span></div>
  </div>
</a>
```

## 7. Output

Deliver to the user, in this order:

1. **Destination**: the exact page URL and whether you're adding to an
   existing page or creating a new one.
2. **Filename**: where the image should be saved (full relative path).
3. **HTML snippet(s)**: exactly what to paste and where.
   - For an existing page: show the `<article class="timeline-entry">`
     block and name the line number or nearest marker to insert before.
   - For a new page: show the full file contents.
   - If landing-page card changes are needed, show those too.
4. **Commit message** in the style of: `feat: add April 16 entry for
   rising-sun-redbud` or `feat: add new plant page for japanese-maple-bloodgood`.

Do NOT invent plant species, cultivars, dates, or past history.
Do NOT change the site voice or color palette mid-entry.
Do NOT add entries to pages whose subject doesn't match the image.

If anything is ambiguous, ask before acting.

## 8. Image considerations

- Expected aspect ratio: photos are displayed at 4:5 or 16:10 crops
  (controlled by CSS `--photo-aspect`).
- Large images are fine — no resize required on your end.
- Alt text: describe what's in the frame in 6-12 words. Accessibility
  matters, so avoid "image of" / "photo of" prefixes.

## 9. Related files

- `content/site.json` — featured cards on the home page. Only touch if
  the new plant is especially featured-worthy.
- `garden/index.html` — the practical calendar. Only touch if the entry
  is about a task/timing, not a photo.
- `assets/plant-page.css` and `assets/design.css` — site-wide styles,
  do not modify without reason.
